name: Lillian "Teddi" Bradford
focus: Maya archaeology • Land use and Settlement Organization • Archaeological Remote Sensing
bio: Lillian M. Bradford is a Masters' student in Anthropology at Northern Arizona University whose work focuses on archaeology, ancient Maya land systems, and human-environment interactions. Her research explores methods for standardizing and synthesizing site maps and remote sensing data, primarily from lidar mapping efforts, to compare ancient land use practices at archaeological sites across southern Mexico, northern Guatemala, and Belize. Her work also involves comparing characteristics of the physical environment with land use practices to further understand Maya settlements as larger social-ecological systems. Bradford seeks to advance methods in archaeology for comparing settlement systems across multiple spatial and temporal scales and to contribute to conversations about the long-term impacts of humans on Earth systems.
